sourceMovie.title: Pineapple Express
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pineapple Express (2008) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Gordon Green's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Crows Zero
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pineapple Express clicked for you, Crows Zero (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pineapple Express clicked for you, Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 16 Blocks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pineapple Express clicked for you, 16 Blocks (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pineapple Express?
faq.answer: Try Crows Zero, Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels, 16 Blocks—each shares DNA with Pineapple Express while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pineapple Express?
faq.answer: Pineapple Express (2008) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pineapple Express worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
